Title: Game icon displays correctly across device resolutions
Description: Preconditions: Game installed on at least 3 different devices with varying screen sizes and resolutions (e.g., small phone, large phone, tablet). Build version confirmed. Rule: 1.1.1
Steps: Install the game on a small-screen device (e.g., 5-inch) -> Game icon appears on home screen | Inspect game icon on small device -> Icon is not pixelated; design matches latest approved document | Install the game on a large-screen device (e.g., 6.7-inch) -> Game icon appears on home screen | Inspect game icon on large device -> Icon is not pixelated; resolution is not negatively affected by larger screen | Inspect game title/name beneath icon -> Text is readable and not cut off or distorted on all tested devices
Expected Result: Game icon is not pixelated, matches latest design document, and displays correctly on all tested screen sizes. Game title text is readable across all device resolutions.

Title: Game icon compatibility between different icon display types
Description: Preconditions: Game installed on at least one device that supports multiple icon display modes (e.g., Rog Phone 5s with Armory Crate, Samsung S21). Rule: 1.1.2
Steps: On a compatible device, navigate to the home screen icon settings -> Icon settings panel opens | Switch icon display type (e.g., from standard to large/small/outlined) -> Setting changes successfully | Return to home screen and inspect game icon -> No display issues (stretching, cropping, pixelation) occur | Repeat the icon type switch for all available types on the device -> Icon renders correctly in each available mode
Expected Result: Game icon displays without any graphical issues across all supported icon display types and sizes on compatible devices.

Title: Complete removal of all game data on uninstall
Description: Preconditions: Game installed and launched at least once. Player has made progress (e.g., completed a level) and changed at least one setting. Local save data exists. Rule: 1.2.1
Steps: Navigate to device Settings > Apps > [Game Name] -> App info screen opens | Tap 'Uninstall' -> Confirmation dialog appears | Confirm uninstallation -> Game is removed from the device | Navigate to device storage (Settings > Storage) and check for any remaining game folders -> No residual game folders exist | Reinstall the game -> Fresh install with no previous data or settings pre-loaded | Launch the game and verify no previous progress or settings are present -> Game starts as a completely new install with no saved data
Expected Result: The uninstall process completely removes the game application and all locally stored data including save files, settings, and temporary files. Reinstalling results in a completely fresh state with no prior data.

Title: Game launches without crash or freeze
Description: Preconditions: Game is freshly installed on the test device. Device has adequate free storage (minimum 500MB free). Build version: [specify build]. Rule: 1.3.1
Steps: Tap the game icon on the home screen -> Game begins to load; no immediate crash occurs | Wait for the game to fully load to first interactive screen -> Game reaches first interactive screen without freezing or crashing | Tap the back button or use the dedicated close function within the game to exit -> Game closes smoothly without delay or force-close error
Expected Result: Game launches successfully without any critical issues (crash, freeze). Game also exits smoothly using the dedicated exit function. No ANR (Application Not Responding) dialog appears.

Title: Landscape-only game does not launch in portrait orientation
Description: Preconditions: Game is confirmed to support Landscape orientation only per GDD. Test device auto-rotate is enabled. Rule: 1.3.2
Steps: Hold the device in portrait orientation -> Device auto-rotate is active | Tap the game icon to launch -> Game launches in landscape orientation regardless of device portrait orientation | Rotate device to landscape orientation -> Game remains in landscape orientation, no visual issues | Attempt to force portrait by holding the device upright and shaking or rotating quickly -> Game remains locked in landscape, no orientation flicker
Expected Result: A landscape-only game always launches and remains in landscape orientation. The game does not switch to portrait mode under any physical orientation change of the device.

Title: Total launch time to first interactive screen does not exceed 30 seconds
Description: Preconditions: Game is freshly installed with no cached data. Test device meets minimum spec. Timer ready. Rule: 1.3.3
Steps: Start a stopwatch simultaneously with tapping the game icon -> Timer starts | Wait for the first interactive menu/screen to appear -> Game displays first interactive screen | Stop the stopwatch immediately when the first interactive element is tappable -> Record elapsed time | Verify recorded time -> Time elapsed is 30 seconds or less
Expected Result: Time elapsed from tapping the game icon to the first interactive screen appearing is 30 seconds or less. No loading hang or frozen screen occurs during this period.

Title: Time between each launch screen transition does not exceed 10 seconds
Description: Preconditions: Game installed. Tester has a stopwatch. This applies to transitions between popups/screens during launch sequence (e.g., popup 1 -> accept -> popup 2 -> accept -> main menu). Rule: 1.3.4
Steps: Launch the game -> First launch screen appears | Start stopwatch when the first popup/screen appears -> Stopwatch running | Tap 'Accept' or equivalent button on the first popup -> Second popup or screen begins loading | Stop stopwatch when the second screen is fully interactive -> Record time; must be 10 seconds or less | Repeat timing for each subsequent screen transition until reaching main menu or gameplay -> Each transition time is 10 seconds or less
Expected Result: Each transition between screens/popups during the launch sequence completes within 10 seconds. No single transition exceeds the 10-second pacing standard.

Title: Asset downloads for liveops trigger after reaching playable area
Description: Preconditions: Game requires downloadable liveops/event assets. Device connected to Wi-Fi. Network monitoring tool active (e.g., device developer options > network stats). Rule: 1.3.5
Steps: Launch the game -> Game begins loading | Observe network activity during initial load -> No large asset downloads occur before reaching the playable area | Reach the first playable area (main menu or gameplay) -> Game is playable and interactive | Monitor network activity after reaching the playable area -> Background asset download begins without interrupting gameplay
Expected Result: Liveops/event asset downloads do not delay the initial launch. Downloads begin only after the user has reached the playable area and do not interrupt gameplay or cause frame drops.

Title: Game launch time complies with standards when using Android game launcher (Android only)
Description: Preconditions: Android device with a supported game launcher (Xiaomi: Game Turbo / Huawei: Huawei Game Center / Rog Phone: Armory Crate). Game added to the launcher. iOS devices are excluded from this test. Rule: 1.3.6
Steps: Open the device's game launcher (e.g., open 'Game Turbo' on Xiaomi) -> Game launcher opens | Locate the game in the launcher library -> Game is listed | Tap the game from within the launcher -> Game begins to launch | Start stopwatch when game icon is tapped in launcher -> Timer starts | Stop stopwatch when first interactive screen appears -> Elapsed time must be 30 seconds or less per rule 1.3.3 | Repeat launch 3 times and record times -> All launch times are within acceptable range
Expected Result: Game launch time from within the Android game launcher complies with the same 30-second maximum standard defined in rule 1.3.3. Launch times are consistent across multiple attempts.

Title: Athena logo is the first screen shown on launch (Athena games)
Description: Preconditions: Game is confirmed to be an Athena Studio published title. Game is freshly installed with no cached data. Rule: 2.1.1
Steps: Tap the game icon to launch the game -> Game begins loading | Observe the first screen that appears -> The Athena Studio logo is the very first visual screen displayed before any other screen | Verify no other screen, splash, or gameplay appears before the Athena logo -> Athena logo appears first | Allow the logo to complete its display -> Logo transitions to the next screen naturally
Expected Result: The Athena Studio logo is confirmed to be the first screen visible to the user on every launch, including both first launch and subsequent launches.

Title: Non-Athena games do not display Athena logo
Description: Preconditions: Game is confirmed to be a Non-Athena published title (different publisher). Rule: 2.1.1
Steps: Launch the game on the test device -> Game begins loading | Observe all screens during the launch sequence -> Athena Studio logo does not appear at any point | Verify the intended publisher logo appears instead -> Correct publisher logo is displayed as specified by the project team
Expected Result: The Athena Studio logo does not appear anywhere during the launch sequence. The correct publisher logo is displayed as defined by the project GDD.

Title: Logo display duration is reasonable and does not linger excessively
Description: Preconditions: Game installed. Tester has a stopwatch. Check GDD for whether logo can be skipped. Rule: 2.1.1
Steps: Launch the game -> Logo screen appears | Start stopwatch when logo appears -> Timer starts | Observe how long logo stays on screen -> Logo does not linger for an unreasonably long duration (e.g., longer than 5 seconds without user input) | Stop stopwatch when logo disappears or transitions away -> Record duration | If GDD allows skipping: tap the screen during logo display -> Logo is skipped and next screen loads | If GDD does not allow skipping: tap the screen during logo display -> Tap is ignored; logo completes its full display before transitioning
Expected Result: The logo is displayed for a reasonable duration. Skip behavior matches GDD specification. Logo does not remain on screen indefinitely or for an unreasonably long time.

Title: Logo resolution is not negatively affected by different screen sizes
Description: Preconditions: At least 2 devices with different screen sizes/aspect ratios (e.g., standard 16:9 and tall 20:9). Rule: 2.1.1
Steps: Launch the game on a standard aspect ratio device (16:9) -> Logo screen appears | Inspect the logo on this device -> Logo is not pixelated, not stretched, not cropped | Launch the game on a tall aspect ratio device (e.g., 20:9 or 21:9) -> Logo screen appears | Inspect the logo on the tall device -> Logo is not pixelated, not stretched, not cropped | Compare logo appearance across both devices -> Logo maintains correct proportions and quality on all tested screens
Expected Result: The logo displays at proper resolution without pixelation, stretching, or cropping on all tested device screen sizes and aspect ratios.

Title: Loading indicator is clearly visible during game loading
Description: Preconditions: Game installed. Device connectivity normal. Rule: 2.2.1
Steps: Launch the game and observe the loading screen -> A visible loading indicator (e.g., progress bar, spinning icon, 'Loading...' text) appears | Observe the loading indicator throughout the loading process -> Indicator is animated/moving and clearly communicates that the game is loading, not frozen | Verify the indicator is readable against the background -> Indicator has sufficient contrast and is not obscured by other UI elements
Expected Result: A clear loading indicator is visible and animated throughout the loading process, ensuring users can distinguish an active loading state from a frozen or crashed app.

Title: Loading screen to first interactive screen within 10 seconds
Description: Preconditions: Game installed. Device on stable Wi-Fi. Stopwatch ready. Rule: 2.2.1
Steps: Launch the game and wait for loading screen to begin -> Loading screen is active | Start stopwatch when the loading bar/indicator begins -> Timer starts | Wait for the loading to finish and the first interactive screen (Main Menu or gameplay) to appear -> First interactive screen appears | Stop stopwatch immediately when first interactive element is tappable -> Record elapsed time | Verify recorded time -> Loading time is 10 seconds or less
Expected Result: Time from loading screen start to the first interactive screen appearing is 10 seconds or less.

Title: Loading screen clearly prompts user input after loading completes
Description: Preconditions: Game configured to require user tap after loading finishes (e.g., 'Tap to Continue'). Rule: 2.2.1
Steps: Launch the game and wait for the loading screen -> Loading screen appears with loading indicator | Wait for loading to complete -> Loading indicator stops | Verify a clear prompt appears (e.g., 'Tap to Continue' flashing) -> A clear user-input prompt appears; it is animated or flashing to draw attention | Tap anywhere on the screen as instructed -> Game proceeds to the next screen
Expected Result: After loading completes, a clear and obvious prompt tells the user to interact (e.g., tap) to proceed. The prompt is easily noticeable and does not leave users unsure of what to do.

Title: Offline mode skips network-required loading screen with connection popup
Description: Preconditions: Device has no internet connection (Wi-Fi off, mobile data off). Game installed. Rule: 2.2.1
Steps: Turn off Wi-Fi and mobile data on the test device -> Device is in airplane mode or has no connectivity | Launch the game -> Game begins loading | Navigate or attempt to load a section that requires internet (e.g., online leaderboard, event screen) -> A loading screen would normally appear | Verify behavior -> The internet-required loading screen is skipped; an 'Internet connection required' popup appears instead | Tap OK or dismiss the popup -> Game returns to the last available offline screen without crashing
Expected Result: When offline, loading screens that lead to internet-required sections are bypassed. A clear 'Internet connection required' dialog appears instead of an endless loading loop.

Title: Loading timeout prevents infinite loading loop on error
Description: Preconditions: Simulate a slow or failed network connection (e.g., throttle via developer tools, put in airplane mode mid-load). Rule: 2.2.2
Steps: Launch the game on a device with throttled or no internet connection -> Game begins to load | Observe the loading screen when it cannot fetch required data -> Loading screen appears but data cannot be retrieved | Wait for timeout behavior to trigger -> After a reasonable timeout period (e.g., 30 seconds), the game does not remain stuck in the loading loop | Verify timeout action -> A clear error message or retry prompt appears; the user is not permanently stuck | Tap retry if shown -> Game attempts to reload; no crash occurs
Expected Result: A loading timeout is implemented. When loading fails or stalls, the game does not trap the user in an infinite loop. An error message or retry option is presented after the timeout period.

Title: First interactive screen has clear indication of how to access main menu
Description: Preconditions: Game installed. Applicable if the first screen is not the main menu itself (e.g., a level select or play screen). Rule: 2.3.2
Steps: Launch the game to the first interactive screen -> First interactive screen is visible | Inspect the screen for a clear indication of how to reach the main menu -> A visible indicator (button, icon, or instructional text) showing how to access the main menu is present | Interact with the indicated element -> Main menu opens or navigates correctly
Expected Result: The first interactive screen clearly shows the user how to access the main menu. No guesswork is required by the user.

Title: Main menu is accessible after forced FTUE tutorial completes
Description: Preconditions: Fresh install with no prior save. Game has a forced FTUE tutorial. Rule: 2.3.2
Steps: Launch the game for the first time -> FTUE tutorial begins automatically | Complete all steps of the forced FTUE tutorial -> Tutorial ends | Verify main menu (or equivalent hub screen) becomes accessible -> Main menu button or navigation option is now visible and tappable | Tap to open main menu -> Main menu opens successfully
Expected Result: The main menu becomes accessible immediately after the forced FTUE tutorial is completed. The main menu is not accessible during the forced tutorial.

Title: Main menu buttons are clearly labeled and graphical assets display correctly
Description: Preconditions: Game at main menu. Rule: 2.3.3
Steps: Navigate to the main menu -> Main menu is displayed | Inspect each button on the main menu -> Each button has a clear label or icon that immediately communicates its function without ambiguity | Check for the Settings button -> Settings button is present and accessible in the main menu | Inspect all graphical assets (backgrounds, icons, banners) on the main menu -> All assets load correctly with no missing textures, overlapping elements, or graphical glitches | Check text on main menu -> All text is free of truncation, overlap, or rendering issues
Expected Result: All main menu buttons are clearly labeled, use unambiguous icons, and all graphical assets display correctly with no visual issues. A Settings button is present on the main menu.

Title: All game sections are accessible from main menu and main menu is accessible from all sections
Description: Preconditions: All game sections unlocked or accessible for testing. Player at main menu. Rule: 2.3.4
Steps: From the main menu, tap each available navigation button (e.g., Play, Shop, Settings, Leaderboard) -> Each section opens correctly without crash | From each opened section, navigate back to the main menu using the back or home button -> Main menu is accessible from every section | Verify transitions between main menu and all sections -> Transitions are smooth; no FPS drop or stuttering occurs during navigation | Verify no section is unreachable from the main menu -> All major game sections are accessible
Expected Result: All sections of the game are reachable from the main menu, and the main menu is reachable from all sections. All transitions are smooth and do not cause any frame rate drops.

Title: In-game menu is accessible during gameplay with clear indicator
Description: Preconditions: Player in active gameplay. Rule: 2.4.2
Steps: Start a gameplay session -> Gameplay is active | Inspect the gameplay screen for an in-game menu indicator (e.g., gear icon, pause button) -> A clear, visible indicator for the in-game menu is present on screen | Verify the indicator is not obstructing gameplay elements excessively -> Indicator is small and unobtrusive but still visible | Tap the in-game menu indicator -> In-game menu opens successfully
Expected Result: A clear, unobtrusive indicator for the in-game menu is always visible during gameplay. The indicator is tappable and opens the in-game menu.

Title: In-game menu indicator has sufficient tap area across different device sizes
Description: Preconditions: Test on at least one small device (5-inch) and one large device (6.7-inch). Player in active gameplay. Rule: 2.4.2
Steps: Start gameplay on a small device (5-inch) -> Gameplay active | Locate the in-game menu indicator -> Indicator is visible | Attempt to tap the indicator with a normal finger tap (not precisely aimed) -> In-game menu opens; tap is registered without requiring very precise input | Repeat on a large device (6.7-inch) -> In-game menu indicator has adequate tap area on large screen as well | Verify the indicator is consistently visible at all screen resolutions and aspect ratios -> No resolutions cause the indicator to be clipped, hidden, or too small to tap
Expected Result: The in-game menu indicator has an adequate tap area on all tested device sizes and is consistently visible and functional across different resolutions and aspect ratios.

Title: Opening in-game menu pauses the game (for timer-based games)
Description: Preconditions: Game includes a countdown timer or real-time mechanic during gameplay. Player in active gameplay with visible timer. Rule: 2.4.4
Steps: Start a gameplay session with an active countdown timer -> Timer is counting down | Note the current timer value -> Timer value recorded | Tap the in-game menu indicator -> In-game menu opens | Observe the timer while the in-game menu is open -> Timer is paused and not counting down | Close the in-game menu -> Gameplay resumes | Verify the timer resumes from the paused value -> Timer continues from the same value it was at when the menu was opened
Expected Result: Opening the in-game menu pauses the gameplay timer immediately. The timer resumes from the correct value when the menu is closed.

Title: In-game menu changes are auto-saved
Description: Preconditions: Game at in-game menu. Player modifies a setting (e.g., toggles sound off). Rule: 2.4.4
Steps: Open the in-game menu during gameplay -> In-game menu opens | Toggle a setting (e.g., turn Sound OFF) -> Setting changes visually | Close the in-game menu without explicitly saving -> In-game menu closes | Continue gameplay briefly -> Game plays with Sound OFF | Force-close the application (swipe away from recent apps) -> App is closed | Relaunch the game -> Game launches | Navigate to in-game menu and check the Sound setting -> Sound setting is still OFF; the change was auto-saved
Expected Result: Changes made in the in-game menu are automatically saved. Settings persist after closing the app and relaunching, without requiring the user to manually save.

Title: In-game menu closes when conflicting tutorial or forced popup appears
Description: Preconditions: Game has a forced tutorial or popup that triggers mid-gameplay. In-game menu is open. Rule: 2.4.2
Steps: Start a gameplay session and open the in-game menu -> In-game menu is open | Trigger a forced tutorial or required popup (this may require using cheat/debug tools to trigger) -> Forced popup or tutorial begins to appear | Observe in-game menu behavior -> In-game menu closes automatically to give priority to the forced tutorial/popup | Verify the tutorial/popup is fully visible and interactive -> Tutorial/popup is unobstructed and functional | Complete the tutorial/popup -> Game resumes normally
Expected Result: When a forced tutorial or higher-priority popup is triggered, the in-game menu closes automatically. The tutorial/popup takes full priority and is fully visible and functional.

Title: All in-game menu items lead to a valid function (no orphan menu items)
Description: Preconditions: In-game menu accessible. All menu items visible. Rule: 2.4.3
Steps: Open the in-game menu -> In-game menu is visible | Tap each menu item one by one -> Each menu item responds with a valid action (opens a sub-menu, triggers a setting, navigates to a screen) | Verify no menu item produces no response, shows a broken/empty screen, or leads to a dead end -> All menu items have functional destinations | Verify all menu item labels are informative and not vague (e.g., not 'Option 1' or '??') -> Labels clearly communicate each item's function
Expected Result: Every in-game menu item leads to a valid, functional action. No menu orphans (items with no function) exist. All labels are clear and informative.

Title: In-game menu opens and closes without FPS drop or stutter
Description: Preconditions: Player in active gameplay. FPS counter active (via developer options or device overlay). Rule: 2.4.4
Steps: Start gameplay and note the stable FPS (e.g., 60fps) -> Baseline FPS recorded | Open the in-game menu -> In-game menu opens | Observe FPS during menu open transition -> FPS does not drop noticeably below the stable baseline | Close the in-game menu -> Menu closes | Observe FPS during menu close transition -> FPS returns to baseline immediately with no sustained drop | Repeat open/close 5 times rapidly -> No stuttering, frame freeze, or persistent FPS drop occurs
Expected Result: Opening and closing the in-game menu does not cause any noticeable FPS drop, stutter, or frame freeze. Gameplay performance is unaffected by menu transitions.

Title: Settings menu is accessible from both main menu and in-game menu
Description: Preconditions: Player has access to both main menu and in-game menu. Rule: 3.1.1
Steps: Navigate to the main menu -> Main menu is visible | Locate and tap the Settings button/icon on the main menu -> Settings menu opens | Close the Settings menu and return to main menu -> Main menu visible | Start a gameplay session and open the in-game menu -> In-game menu is open | Locate and tap the Settings option within the in-game menu -> Settings menu opens from within in-game menu | Verify Settings menu content is identical from both access points -> Same settings are available regardless of entry point
Expected Result: The Settings menu is accessible from both the Main Menu and the In-game Menu. The content available in Settings is consistent regardless of the access point.

Title: Current status of each setting is clearly displayed
Description: Preconditions: Open Settings menu. Rule: 3.1.2
Steps: Open the Settings menu from either Main Menu or In-game Menu -> Settings menu is visible | Inspect each setting toggle/slider/option -> The current state of each setting is clearly indicated (e.g., toggle shows ON/OFF, slider shows current value position, checkbox is checked/unchecked) | Toggle a setting (e.g., turn Sound from ON to OFF) -> Setting visually updates to reflect the new state immediately | Verify the displayed state matches the actual game behavior -> If Sound is shown as OFF, no audio plays in-game
Expected Result: The current state of every setting is clearly and accurately displayed within the Settings menu. Visual indicators update immediately when a setting is changed.

Title: Each setting is modifiable independently without predefined combinations
Description: Preconditions: Settings menu open with at least Sound and Vibration settings available. Rule: 3.1.3
Steps: Open the Settings menu -> Settings menu visible | Set Sound to ON and Vibration to ON -> Both settings are ON | Set Sound to OFF while keeping Vibration ON -> Sound turns OFF; Vibration remains ON with no automatic change | Verify in-game audio is muted while vibration still works -> Sound is off; vibration (if triggered by gameplay) still functions | Return to Settings and set Vibration to OFF while keeping Sound OFF -> Vibration turns OFF; Sound remains OFF | Set Sound back to ON while Vibration is OFF -> Sound turns ON; Vibration remains OFF independently
Expected Result: Sound and Vibration settings can each be enabled or disabled independently. No predefined combinations force one setting to change when the other is modified.

Title: Changing a setting does not affect core game functionality
Description: Preconditions: Settings menu accessible. Player has completed at least one gameplay session to establish baseline. Rule: 3.1.3
Steps: Open Settings and toggle Sound to OFF -> Sound is turned off | Close Settings and start a gameplay session -> Gameplay starts normally | Verify core gameplay functions correctly with Sound OFF -> Gameplay mechanics (score, timer, collision, win/lose) all function identically to Sound ON state | Return to Settings and toggle Vibration to OFF -> Vibration is turned off | Start another gameplay session -> Core gameplay mechanics function identically | Toggle all available settings to non-default values and run a full gameplay session -> Core gameplay is unaffected by any setting combinations
Expected Result: No combination of settings changes causes any degradation or alteration of the core gameplay functionality. Turning off Sound or Vibration affects only audio/haptics, not gameplay execution.

Title: Game version number is visible within the game
Description: Preconditions: Settings menu accessible. Game build version known (e.g., v1.2.3). Rule: 3.1.4
Steps: Navigate to Settings > [About/Info or main Settings page] -> Settings is open | Search for the game version number display -> Game version number is visible (e.g., 'v1.2.3' or 'Build 123') | If version is not in Settings, check the loading screen -> Version number is displayed on the loading/splash screen | Verify the displayed version matches the build being tested -> Version number matches the test build specification
Expected Result: The game version number is visible somewhere within the game (in Settings, loading screen, or both). The displayed version matches the actual test build being evaluated.

Title: User ID is visible in Settings for cloud-save enabled games
Description: Preconditions: Game supports cloud saving (e.g., Google Play Games login, Facebook login). User is logged in. Rule: 3.1.4
Steps: Log in to the game using the cloud save account (Google/Facebook) -> User is logged in | Navigate to Settings menu -> Settings menu open | Locate the User ID display -> User ID is visible in Settings (or a dedicated account/profile section) | Verify the displayed User ID matches the logged-in account ID -> User ID is accurate and corresponds to the logged-in account
Expected Result: For games with cloud save functionality, the logged-in User ID is clearly visible within the Settings menu or account section.

Title: Toggling sound from OFF to ON provides immediate audio feedback
Description: Preconditions: Sound setting is currently OFF. Device physical volume is at a level that allows audio (not silenced). Rule: 3.2.1
Steps: Open Settings menu -> Settings visible | Confirm Sound setting is currently showing as OFF -> Sound is OFF | Toggle Sound setting from OFF to ON -> Sound toggle moves to ON | Listen immediately after the toggle -> An audio feedback sound plays immediately confirming that sound is now ON
Expected Result: An audio feedback sound (e.g., a short chime or game SFX) plays immediately when Sound is toggled from OFF to ON.

Title: Toggling sound from ON to OFF produces no audio
Description: Preconditions: Sound setting is currently ON. Device physical volume is at audible level. Rule: 3.2.1
Steps: Open Settings menu -> Settings visible | Confirm Sound setting is currently ON -> Sound is ON | Toggle Sound setting from ON to OFF -> Sound toggle moves to OFF | Listen immediately after toggle and during subsequent interactions -> No sound plays at the moment of toggling or during any gameplay/UI interaction while Sound is OFF
Expected Result: No audio plays when Sound is toggled from ON to OFF, and no audio plays while the Sound setting remains OFF.

Title: Toggling vibration from OFF to ON triggers a short haptic response
Description: Preconditions: Vibration setting is currently OFF. Device supports haptic feedback. Rule: 3.2.2
Steps: Open Settings menu -> Settings visible | Confirm Vibration setting is currently OFF -> Vibration is OFF | Toggle Vibration from OFF to ON -> Vibration toggle moves to ON | Feel the device immediately after toggle -> A short vibration activates on the device confirming vibration is now ON
Expected Result: A short haptic vibration response occurs immediately when Vibration is toggled from OFF to ON (recommended behavior per rule 3.2.2).

Title: Toggling vibration from ON to OFF produces no haptic response
Description: Preconditions: Vibration setting is currently ON. Rule: 3.2.2
Steps: Open Settings menu -> Settings visible | Confirm Vibration setting is currently ON -> Vibration is ON | Toggle Vibration from ON to OFF -> Vibration toggle moves to OFF | Interact with gameplay or UI buttons that normally trigger vibration -> No vibration occurs during any interaction while Vibration is set to OFF
Expected Result: No haptic vibration occurs when Vibration is toggled to OFF, and no vibration occurs during any gameplay or UI interactions while the Vibration setting remains OFF.

Title: Phone volume OFF + in-game Sound ON results in no audio output
Description: Preconditions: Device physical volume set to 0 (muted via hardware button). In-game Sound setting is ON. Rule: 3.2.3
Steps: Set device physical volume to 0 (completely muted) using hardware volume buttons -> Device is muted | Open game Settings and verify in-game Sound is set to ON -> In-game Sound is ON | Start a gameplay session -> Gameplay begins | Trigger audio events (e.g., tap buttons, start combat) -> No audio plays despite in-game Sound being ON, because device volume is 0
Expected Result: With device volume at 0, no game audio plays regardless of in-game Sound setting being ON. Device mute overrides in-game Sound setting.

Title: Phone volume ON + in-game Sound OFF results in no audio output
Description: Preconditions: Device physical volume set to a non-zero level (audible). In-game Sound setting is OFF. Rule: 3.2.3
Steps: Set device physical volume to 50% or higher using hardware buttons -> Device volume is audible | Open game Settings and verify in-game Sound is set to OFF -> In-game Sound is OFF | Start a gameplay session -> Gameplay begins | Trigger audio events (e.g., tap buttons, win a level) -> No game audio plays despite device volume being ON
Expected Result: With in-game Sound set to OFF, no game audio plays regardless of the device physical volume level. In-game Sound OFF overrides device volume.

Title: Phone volume ON + in-game Sound ON results in audible game sounds
Description: Preconditions: Device physical volume set to an audible level. In-game Sound setting is ON. Rule: 3.2.3
Steps: Set device physical volume to 50% using hardware buttons -> Device volume is audible | Open game Settings and verify in-game Sound is set to ON -> In-game Sound is ON | Start a gameplay session -> Gameplay begins | Trigger various audio events (tap UI buttons, win/lose a level, trigger SFX) -> Game sounds are clearly audible | Adjust device volume up and down -> Game audio volume changes proportionally with device volume | Verify audio is not distorted at any volume level -> Audio is clear at all tested volume levels
Expected Result: When both device volume and in-game Sound are ON, game audio is clearly audible. Audio volume adjusts proportionally with the device's physical volume. No audio distortion occurs.

Title: Cheat/debug menu is present and accessible in test builds
Description: Preconditions: Test build of the game installed (not production build). Tester has cheat menu access method from dev team. Rule: 3.3.1
Steps: Access the cheat menu using the provided access method (e.g., tap logo 7 times, use debug console command, long-press on version number) -> Cheat menu appears | Verify the cheat menu opens without crashing the app -> Cheat menu is functional and stable | Confirm the cheat menu is NOT accessible in production builds (if applicable) -> Cheat menu is hidden/disabled in production
Expected Result: The cheat/debug menu is accessible in test builds via the provided method. The app does not crash when accessing the cheat menu.

Title: Win condition cheat triggers instant win correctly
Description: Preconditions: Cheat menu accessible. Player is in an active gameplay session (mid-level). Rule: 3.3.2
Steps: Start a gameplay session and reach mid-level (do not complete it naturally) -> Gameplay active, level not won | Open the cheat menu -> Cheat menu opens | Activate the 'Enable win condition instantly' cheat -> Cheat activated | Observe game behavior -> Win condition triggers immediately; win screen or level completion sequence plays | Verify correct win rewards/progression are granted -> Level marked as complete; rewards (if any) granted appropriately
Expected Result: The instant win cheat immediately triggers the win condition from any point in gameplay. The win sequence plays correctly and progress is updated.

Title: Currency cheat correctly increases and decreases in-game currency
Description: Preconditions: Cheat menu accessible. Player has a known amount of in-game currency (e.g., 100 coins). Rule: 3.3.2
Steps: Note current in-game currency amount (e.g., 100 coins) -> Starting amount recorded | Open cheat menu and use 'Increase currency' cheat (e.g., add 1000 coins) -> Cheat activated | Check in-game currency display -> Currency increased to expected amount (e.g., 1100 coins) | Open cheat menu and use 'Decrease currency' cheat (e.g., subtract 500 coins) -> Cheat activated | Check in-game currency display -> Currency decreased to expected amount (e.g., 600 coins) | Verify currency cannot be decreased below 0 by cheat -> Currency floors at 0, no negative values appear
Expected Result: The currency cheat accurately increases and decreases the in-game currency by the specified amounts. Currency cannot be reduced below zero.

Title: Debug console enable/disable functions correctly
Description: Preconditions: Cheat menu accessible. Rule: 3.3.2
Steps: Open cheat menu -> Cheat menu visible | Activate 'Enable debug console' -> Debug console overlay appears on screen | Verify the debug console displays relevant game data (e.g., FPS, memory, logs) -> Console shows useful debug information | Activate 'Disable debug console' from cheat menu -> Debug console overlay disappears | Verify game performance is not affected by toggling debug console -> FPS and gameplay remain stable with console both on and off
Expected Result: The debug console can be enabled and disabled via the cheat menu. When enabled, it displays relevant debug information without crashing the game. Disabling it removes the overlay cleanly.

Title: Info & Support section is accessible within the game
Description: Preconditions: Game installed. Player on main menu or in-game menu. Rule: 3.4.1
Steps: Navigate to the main menu -> Main menu visible | Look for an Info & Support section (dedicated icon, button, or within Settings) -> Info & Support entry point is identifiable | Tap the Info & Support entry -> Info & Support section opens without crash | Verify all required links/content are present -> Privacy policy, Terms of Use, and Support link/email are present
Expected Result: An Info & Support section is present within the game and accessible via a dedicated icon or within an appropriate menu. The section does not crash on open.

Title: Privacy policy link is present and opens correctly
Description: Preconditions: Player has access to Info & Support section. Device has internet access. Rule: 3.4.2, 7.0.1, 7.0.2
Steps: Navigate to Info & Support section -> Section is open | Locate the Privacy Policy link -> Privacy Policy link is present and visible | Tap the Privacy Policy link -> Device browser opens | Verify the URL that opens matches the correct publisher's privacy policy URL -> Correct URL loads (e.g., https://athena.studio/privacy-policy for Athena games) | Verify the page loads correctly and is not a 404 or broken page -> Privacy policy content is visible and readable
Expected Result: The Privacy Policy link is present, tappable, and opens the correct publisher-specific privacy policy URL in a browser. The page loads without error.

Title: Terms of Service link is present and opens correctly
Description: Preconditions: Player has access to Info & Support section. Device has internet access. Rule: 3.4.2, 7.0.1, 7.0.2
Steps: Navigate to Info & Support section -> Section is open | Locate the Terms of Service/Use link -> ToS link is present and visible | Tap the Terms of Service link -> Device browser opens | Verify the URL matches the correct publisher's ToS URL -> Correct URL loads (e.g., https://athena.studio/term for Athena games) | Verify the page loads correctly -> ToS content is visible and not a broken page
Expected Result: The Terms of Service link is present, tappable, and opens the correct publisher-specific ToS URL. The page loads correctly.

Title: All in-game text is free of grammatical and spelling errors
Description: Preconditions: Access to all in-game screens. Language set to English (primary). Rule: 4.1.1
Steps: Navigate through all major screens: Main Menu, Settings, Gameplay UI, Win/Lose screen, Shop, Popups -> All screens visited | Read every visible text element on each screen -> All text is grammatically correct and free of spelling errors | Check tutorial text and instructional prompts -> Tutorial text is clear, coherent, and free of errors | Check all popup messages and dialogs -> All popup text is grammatically correct | Flag any text that appears incorrect or unclear -> All flagged text reported
Expected Result: All in-game text is free of grammatical errors, spelling mistakes, and incoherent phrasing across all screens, menus, and UI elements.

Title: Text is legible and does not overflow or clip on all device sizes
Description: Preconditions: Test on small device (5-inch) and large device (6.7-inch). Rule: 4.1.1
Steps: Launch game on small device and navigate through all major screens -> Game running on small device | Inspect all text for readability -> Text is legible; font size is appropriate for the screen | Check for any text that is cut off or overflows its container -> No text overflow or clipping on small device | Repeat on large device -> Game running on large device | Inspect all text -> Text is legible and fits within all text containers on large device | Check for any text that is stretched or has excessive white space -> No text rendering issues on large device
Expected Result: All in-game text is legible with an appropriate font size on all tested device sizes. Text does not overflow, clip, or become unreadable at any tested resolution.

Title: Localized text uses correct accents and grammar for each language
Description: Preconditions: Game supports multiple languages. Test with at least one non-English language (e.g., French, Spanish, Portuguese). Rule: 4.1.1
Steps: Navigate to Settings > Language and switch to French -> Language switches to French | Review all main menu text, button labels, and popups in French -> French text uses correct accents (é, è, ê, ç, etc.) | Check French text for grammar correctness -> Phrasing follows French grammar rules | Switch to Spanish -> Language switches to Spanish | Review all text in Spanish -> Spanish text uses correct accents (á, é, í, ó, ú, ñ, etc.) | Check that localized text still fits within text containers in all languages -> No text overflow occurs in any tested language
Expected Result: All localized languages display correct accents, grammar, and orthographic conventions. Text rendering is correct and fits within UI containers for each tested language.

Title: UI and gameplay button colors follow consistent color language
Description: Preconditions: Access to all major screens including main menu, gameplay, win/lose screens, shop. Rule: 4.2.2
Steps: Navigate to the main menu and identify all CTA (Call to Action) buttons (e.g., 'Play', 'Continue', 'Shop') -> All CTA buttons are identified | Verify CTA buttons use the most prominent, eye-catching accent color -> CTA buttons use the brightest/most prominent color on each screen | Identify all confirmation/positive buttons (e.g., 'OK', 'Accept', 'Confirm') -> Positive buttons identified | Verify positive buttons use green or equivalent 'confirm' color -> Color language is consistent | Identify all negative/cancel buttons (e.g., 'No', 'Cancel', 'Exit') -> Negative buttons identified | Verify destructive or 'stop' actions use red sparingly -> Red is used only for destructive actions; not overused to cause constant sense of error | Check that button color does not make text illegible -> All button text has sufficient contrast against its background color
Expected Result: Color usage is consistent throughout the game. CTA buttons use the most prominent accent colors. Positive actions use green-equivalent, negative/destructive actions use red sparingly. No color choice makes text unreadable.

Title: Buttons provide visible feedback on tap (visual press/color change)
Description: Preconditions: Any screen with interactive buttons. Rule: 4.2.3
Steps: Navigate to the main menu -> Main menu visible | Tap any button (e.g., 'Play') and observe visual response -> Button visually responds (e.g., shrinks slightly, changes color, or shows press-down animation) | Tap a settings icon -> Icon shows visual press feedback | Tap UI elements during gameplay -> Each interactive element shows immediate visual feedback | Verify feedback occurs within 1 frame of the tap -> No delay between tap and visual response
Expected Result: Every interactive button and UI element provides immediate visual feedback (press-down animation, color change, or size change) when tapped. No button appears unresponsive or has delayed feedback.

Title: New features are introduced with clear visual signposting
Description: Preconditions: Fresh install or a state where a new feature is being introduced for the first time (e.g., a new game mechanic unlocks). Rule: 4.2.4
Steps: Progress to the point where a new feature or mechanic is introduced -> New feature activation trigger reached | Observe whether a visual signpost appears (e.g., animated hand/finger, highlight, arrow) -> A clear visual cue appears pointing to the new feature | Verify the visual cue demonstrates the required action (e.g., animated swipe gesture shows how to swipe) -> The cue visually demonstrates the exact action required | Interact with the new feature as indicated -> Feature functions correctly after being introduced
Expected Result: New features and mechanics are introduced with clear, animated visual signposting that demonstrates the required action. Players are not left to guess how to interact with new elements.

Title: End-of-content screen shows Coming Soon message
Description: Preconditions: Use cheat menu to reach the last available level/content. Rule: 4.2.9
Steps: Use cheat menu to jump to the final available level -> Final level accessible | Complete the final available level -> Level complete | Observe the screen that appears after completing the last available content -> A 'Coming Soon', 'More levels coming soon', or equivalent message appears | Verify the message is clearly visible and not ambiguous -> Message communicates that more content is on the way | Verify tapping outside the message or pressing back does not crash the app -> App navigates to the appropriate previous screen
Expected Result: After completing all currently available content, a clear 'Coming Soon' or equivalent message informs the user that more content is in development. The app does not crash or show an empty/broken screen.

Title: IAP price, contents, and recurring charges clearly displayed before purchase
Description: Preconditions: Game has at least one IAP offer. Device connected to internet with a test account. Rule: 4.3.1
Steps: Navigate to the in-game Shop -> Shop screen opens | Tap on any IAP offer (e.g., coin bundle, subscription) -> IAP offer detail screen or popup appears | Verify the price is clearly displayed in local currency before the purchase confirmation -> Price is shown in correct local currency format | Verify the contents of the offer are clearly listed -> What is included (coins, items, bonus) is explicitly stated | If the IAP is a subscription, verify recurring charges are disclosed -> Subscription period and recurring charge amount are clearly shown before confirmation | Tap 'Buy' or proceed to purchase confirmation -> App store purchase confirmation dialog appears | Verify the price shown in the app matches the app store price -> Prices match with no discrepancy
Expected Result: All IAP offers clearly display their price (in local currency), all included contents, and any recurring charge information before purchase confirmation. App displayed price matches app store price.

Title: No deceptive pricing or dark patterns in IAP flow
Description: Preconditions: Navigate through the entire IAP purchase flow for multiple offer types. Rule: 4.3.1
Steps: Browse all available IAP offers in the Shop -> All offers visible | Inspect each offer for any misleading price display (e.g., crossed-out fake original price that was never real, hidden fees) -> No deceptive pricing found | Check for dark patterns (e.g., pre-selected expensive options, confusing 'No' button placement, purchase button that looks like a close button) -> No dark patterns identified | Check promotional offers (e.g., 'Sale! 50% OFF!') -> All promotional offers clearly state their end date or conditions | Verify limited-time offers show a countdown or clear expiration -> Duration/condition of all promotional offers is clearly displayed
Expected Result: No deceptive pricing, false discounts, or dark UI patterns are present in any IAP flow. All promotional claims are clearly qualified with accurate conditions and durations.

Title: Interstitial ads do not appear immediately on app launch
Description: Preconditions: Game with ad integration. Player account without ad-removal purchase. Rule: 4.3.2
Steps: Launch the game from the home screen -> Game loads to first interactive screen | Observe the first 30 seconds after reaching the main menu -> No interstitial ad appears immediately on launch | Navigate quickly between menus (Main Menu -> Settings -> Main Menu) -> No interstitial ad appears during quick menu navigation | Complete the first gameplay session -> Interstitial ad may appear after a session, but not during active critical gameplay
Expected Result: No interstitial ad appears immediately upon app launch, during critical gameplay moments, or during quick sequential menu navigations. Ads appear only at appropriate, non-disruptive moments.

Title: Rewarded video ads require explicit user consent before playing
Description: Preconditions: Game has rewarded video ad placements. Player has access to a rewarded ad trigger point (e.g., 'Watch ad to continue', 'Watch ad for bonus coins'). Rule: 4.3.2
Steps: Reach a rewarded ad trigger point (e.g., lose a level and see 'Watch ad to continue') -> Rewarded ad prompt appears | Verify the prompt clearly communicates what reward will be received -> Reward description is explicit (e.g., '+500 coins' or 'Continue for free') | Verify the player must actively tap a button to consent to watching the ad -> No ad auto-plays; user must explicitly tap to watch | Tap the 'Watch Ad' button -> Rewarded video ad begins playing | Allow the ad to finish -> Ad completes; reward is granted | Alternatively, tap the 'No thanks' or close button -> Ad does not play; reward is not granted; game continues without forcing the ad
Expected Result: Rewarded ads are only triggered by explicit user consent via a clear tap. The reward is clearly communicated before watching. Players can decline without penalty. Ad plays only after affirmative user action.

Title: Ad content does not contain sexual, gambling, or gory material
Description: Preconditions: Game with ad integration. Monitor all ad content during a minimum 30-minute testing session across multiple sessions. Rule: 4.3.3
Steps: Play through multiple gameplay sessions totaling at least 30 minutes -> Gameplay sessions completed; multiple ads triggered | For each ad shown, observe its content carefully -> Record ad content type, network, and any concerning content | Check for ads containing: sexual or implied sexual content -> None found | Check for ads containing: gambling mechanics or imagery -> None found | Check for ads containing: gory, shocking, or violent imagery -> None found | If any violating ad is encountered: record the app name shown, the ad network name, and the app store link of the advertised app -> Report details documented for PM
Expected Result: No ads containing sexual content, gambling content, or gory/shock content are displayed. Any violating ads encountered are reported with full details (advertised app name, ad network, app store link) to the PM.

Title: Ads do not cause game performance issues
Description: Preconditions: Game with ad integration. FPS monitoring active. Rule: 4.3.2
Steps: Play through gameplay until an interstitial or banner ad loads -> Ad is displayed | Monitor FPS and app responsiveness during ad display -> FPS does not drop below 30fps; app remains responsive | Verify ad loads without causing the game to freeze or stutter -> No freeze or stutter occurs during ad load | Close the ad and return to gameplay -> Gameplay resumes immediately without performance degradation | Repeat for at least 5 ad instances -> All ad instances load and close without causing crashes or persistent performance issues
Expected Result: Ads load and display without causing FPS drops, stuttering, freezing, or crashes. Game performance returns to normal immediately after an ad is dismissed.

Title: Game resumes correctly after incoming phone call
Description: Preconditions: Player mid-gameplay (in an active session with visible progress). A second phone or SIM available to make a test call to the test device. Rule: 5.1.1
Steps: Start an active gameplay session and make notable progress (e.g., reach a specific score, move to a specific position) -> Gameplay active with measurable progress | From a second phone, call the test device -> Incoming call notification appears on test device | Answer the call on the test device -> Call is active; game goes to background | End the call after 10 seconds -> Call ends | Return to the game from the recent apps tray -> Game resumes | Verify game state: player position, score, inventory, timer -> All data matches the state before the interruption; no data loss | Verify no graphical glitches or visual corruption -> Screen displays correctly | If game has looping music: verify music resumes -> Music plays again after interruption
Expected Result: Game fully resumes from the exact point of interruption after an incoming phone call. No data loss, graphical glitches, or audio issues occur. Looping music resumes.

Title: Game resumes correctly after being backgrounded and switched back
Description: Preconditions: Player mid-gameplay with visible progress. Rule: 5.1.1
Steps: Start an active gameplay session and note current game state -> Gameplay active | Press the home button to background the game -> Game goes to background | Open another app (e.g., browser) and use it for 10 seconds -> Different app in use | Return to the game via the recent apps switcher -> Game returns to foreground | Verify game state: player position, score, inventory, timer match the pre-background state -> All data intact with no loss | Verify no graphical glitches or artifacts -> Screen displays correctly | Verify gameplay is immediately responsive (no input lag) -> Game responds to input without delay
Expected Result: Game resumes seamlessly from the exact state it was in before being backgrounded. No data loss, graphical issues, or input lag occurs after switching back from another app.

Title: Game resumes correctly after tapping a notification
Description: Preconditions: Player mid-gameplay. Device has push notifications enabled from another app (e.g., messaging app). Rule: 5.1.1
Steps: Start an active gameplay session and note the current game state -> Gameplay active | Trigger or receive a notification from another app (e.g., a message) -> Notification banner appears at top of screen | Tap the notification banner -> Notification app opens (e.g., Messages) | Navigate back to the game via recent apps -> Game returns to foreground | Verify game state matches the pre-notification state -> All data intact with no loss | Verify no graphical glitches -> Screen displays correctly | Verify the game is immediately playable -> Input is responsive
Expected Result: Game resumes seamlessly after the user taps a notification and returns to the game. All progress and state data are preserved.

Title: System permission dialog pauses game and resumes seamlessly after dismissal
Description: Preconditions: Game triggers a system permission request (e.g., notifications, location). This typically occurs on first launch or first time accessing a feature. Rule: 5.1.2
Steps: Trigger the feature that requires a system permission (e.g., first time receiving a notification prompt) -> System permission dialog appears ('Allow [Game] to send notifications?') | Observe game behavior behind the dialog -> Game is paused or in a stable state; it does not crash or continue uncontrolled | Tap 'Allow' on the system dialog -> Dialog dismissed | Verify game resumes seamlessly with no graphical glitches -> Game resumes correctly | Repeat the test and tap 'Don't Allow' on the dialog -> Dialog dismissed | Verify game resumes seamlessly regardless of the permission choice -> Game resumes correctly whether permission is granted or denied
Expected Result: The game pauses gracefully when a system permission dialog appears. After dismissal (regardless of Allow/Deny choice), gameplay resumes seamlessly with no graphical glitches or unresponsive input.

Title: Push notification tapped from background brings game to foreground correctly
Description: Preconditions: Game is running in the background. Device has game push notifications enabled. A game push notification is sent or triggered. Rule: 5.1.3
Steps: Start a game session and then press Home to background the app -> Game is in background | Wait for or trigger a game push notification to arrive -> Notification appears in notification bar | Tap the game push notification -> Game comes to the foreground | Verify game state: the game resumes at the correct screen (e.g., main menu, or the relevant screen for the notification) -> Game is at the correct screen; not at a blank or crashed state | Verify gameplay is functional and responsive -> Input is accepted; game behaves normally
Expected Result: Tapping a push notification while the game is in the background brings the app to the foreground and resumes the current session seamlessly.

Title: Game loads progress from latest checkpoint after simulated power loss
Description: Preconditions: Player has reached a save checkpoint (e.g., completed a level, triggered an auto-save). Note the exact game state at the checkpoint. Rule: 5.2.1
Steps: Reach a confirmed save point and note the game state (level, inventory, score) -> Save point reached; state documented | Simulate power loss by force-stopping the app (long-press app icon > Force Stop, or hold power button and force shut down) -> App is forcefully terminated | Relaunch the game -> Game launches | Verify the game loads from the latest checkpoint before the forced termination -> Game state matches the documented save point data | Verify no data corruption: character stats, inventory, and level progress are accurate -> All data is correct and uncorrupted | Verify no graphical errors appear on the screen after loading -> Screen displays correctly
Expected Result: After a simulated power loss, the game successfully loads from the most recent checkpoint. All character stats, inventory, and progress data are accurate and uncorrupted.

Title: All modified settings persist correctly after app shutdown and relaunch
Description: Preconditions: Player has access to Settings. Record default setting values. Rule: 5.2.2
Steps: Open Settings and modify multiple settings: turn Sound OFF, turn Vibration OFF, and change any other available setting from its default -> All settings changed from default | Close the Settings menu -> Settings closed | Force-close the application -> App terminated | Relaunch the game -> Game launches | Navigate to Settings -> Settings menu open | Verify all previously changed settings retained their new values: Sound is still OFF, Vibration is still OFF -> All changed settings have been saved and restored correctly | Repeat test with different setting combinations -> Settings persist across all tested combinations
Expected Result: All user-modified settings are saved when the app is closed and correctly restored when the app is relaunched, for all setting combinations tested.

Title: Data deletion requires clear warning and deliberate user action
Description: Preconditions: Game has a data reset or account deletion feature. Player has meaningful game progress to delete. Rule: 5.2.3
Steps: Navigate to the data deletion option (e.g., Settings > Reset Progress > Delete Account) -> Data deletion option found | Tap the delete/reset option -> A clear warning dialog appears before deletion executes | Read the warning dialog -> Warning clearly states what will be permanently deleted and that this action cannot be undone | Verify the warning does not mislead about data recovery (e.g., does not say 'data can be recovered' if it cannot) -> Warning is honest about permanence | To confirm deletion, user must perform a deliberate action (e.g., type 'DELETE', tap a specific 'Yes, Delete' button) -> No accidental deletion possible | Tap 'Cancel' or 'No' -> No data is deleted; game returns to previous screen
Expected Result: Data deletion always shows a clear, prominent warning before execution. The warning accurately describes what will be deleted and whether it is reversible. Deletion requires an explicit, deliberate user confirmation action. Cancellation results in no data loss.

Title: Game launches and basic gameplay is available in offline mode
Description: Preconditions: Device in airplane mode (Wi-Fi and mobile data OFF). Game previously installed with local save data present. Rule: 6.0.1
Steps: Enable airplane mode on the test device -> Device has no internet connection | Launch the game -> Game loads without crash | Verify the game reaches a playable state (main menu or gameplay) -> Game is playable offline | Start a gameplay session -> Gameplay begins and is functional | Complete a level or gameplay session -> Level completes; progress is saved locally | Verify local save functions correctly while offline -> Progress is saved; no error about cloud sync during gameplay
Expected Result: The game launches, reaches a playable state, and allows basic gameplay including local progress saving while in offline (airplane) mode.

Title: Game continues without interruption after losing internet connection mid-gameplay
Description: Preconditions: Game running with active internet connection. Player in active gameplay session. Rule: 6.0.2
Steps: Start an active gameplay session with internet connection -> Gameplay active | Mid-gameplay, disable Wi-Fi (Settings > Wi-Fi > Off) -> Internet connection lost | Observe game behavior immediately after connection loss -> Game continues without crashing or freezing | Verify no pop-up or dialog appears informing the user of the connection loss during gameplay -> No interrupting popup appears | Continue playing for 60 seconds after connection loss -> Gameplay continues normally for the full 60 seconds | Verify no error message or notification interrupts the gameplay session -> Session plays through without forced disconnection
Expected Result: Losing internet connectivity mid-gameplay does not interrupt, crash, or pause the game. No popup or notification appears to inform the user of the connection loss during active gameplay.

Title: Online feature shows clear error message when accessed without internet
Description: Preconditions: Device in offline mode (airplane mode). Game at main menu. Game has at least one online-only feature (e.g., leaderboard, cloud sync, online events). Rule: 6.0.3
Steps: Enable airplane mode -> Device is offline | Launch the game -> Game launches offline | Navigate to an online-only feature (e.g., Leaderboard, Online Events tab) -> Attempt to access the feature | Observe the displayed message -> A clear, user-friendly error message appears explaining that an internet connection is required | Verify the error message avoids technical jargon -> Message uses plain language (e.g., 'No internet connection. Please check your network settings.') | Tap OK or dismiss the error -> Message dismisses; game returns to previous screen without crash
Expected Result: Attempting to access any online-only feature while offline displays a clear, plain-language error message. The message avoids jargon. Dismissing the error returns the user to the previous screen without crashing.

Title: Game auto-reconnects after network is restored without requiring restart
Description: Preconditions: Game running in offline mode. Player on main menu or in gameplay. Rule: 6.0.4
Steps: Launch the game in offline mode (airplane mode ON) -> Game is running offline | Re-enable Wi-Fi or disable airplane mode -> Internet connection becomes available | Observe game behavior after reconnection -> Game does not crash; gameplay is unaffected | After reconnection, attempt to access an online feature (e.g., Leaderboard, Shop) -> Online feature loads correctly within a reasonable timeframe (e.g., under 10 seconds) | Verify the app did not need to be restarted to reconnect -> Game reconnected automatically without requiring manual restart
Expected Result: After network connectivity is restored, the game automatically reconnects and online features become accessible within a reasonable timeframe without requiring the user to restart the app.

Title: Consent popup appears on first launch and cannot be skipped
Description: Preconditions: Fresh install of the game (no previous data). GDPR/CCPA consent mechanism expected. Rule: 7.0.3
Steps: Install the game for the first time -> Fresh install complete | Launch the game -> Game begins to load | Observe first launch sequence -> A consent/privacy notice popup appears before the player can interact with any game content | Attempt to skip the consent popup by tapping outside it or pressing Back -> Popup cannot be dismissed or bypassed; it must be interacted with | Verify the popup clearly explains what data will be collected -> Privacy notice uses clear, understandable language | Verify the popup provides options to accept or decline specific data collection categories -> User can make granular choices (not just a blanket 'Accept All')
Expected Result: A clear, non-skippable consent popup appears on first launch, blocking access to game content until the user makes an explicit choice. The popup explains data collection and offers meaningful accept/decline options.

Title: User can modify data consent settings within the game at any time
Description: Preconditions: Game previously installed, consent given on first launch. Rule: 7.0.3
Steps: Launch the game (not first launch; consent already given) -> Game launches to main menu | Navigate to Settings > [Privacy / Data / Consent] section -> Privacy/consent settings section found | Verify the user can modify their data collection preferences -> Toggles or options for different data categories are present and modifiable | Change a consent preference (e.g., disable analytics tracking) -> Preference updates and saves | Force-close and relaunch the app -> App relaunches | Return to Privacy/consent settings -> The modified consent preference is still saved correctly
Expected Result: Users can access and modify their data collection consent preferences at any time from within the game settings. Changes are saved and persist across sessions.

Title: Correct publisher-specific Privacy Policy and ToS URLs are used
Description: Preconditions: Know which publisher the game belongs to (Athena, MG Soft, Tekifun). Device has internet access. Rule: 7.0.2
Steps:
Navigate to Info & Support or Settings within the game -> Relevant section open | Tap the Privacy Policy link -> Browser opens | Verify the URL matches the publisher's correct link:
- Athena games: https://athena.studio/privacy-policy
- MG Soft games: https://sites.google.com/view/mgsoftgame/privacy-policy
- Tekifun games: https://saola.app/privacy-policy/ -> URL is correct for this game's publisher | Navigate back and tap the Terms of Service link -> Browser opens | Verify the ToS URL is correct for this publisher:
- Athena games: https://athena.studio/term -> URL matches expected ToS link for this publisher | Verify both pages load successfully and display actual policy content -> Pages are not 404 or blank
Expected Result: The Privacy Policy and Terms of Service links use the correct publisher-specific URLs. Both URLs load valid, readable content without errors.
